steps.title: Link observation to theory
steps.content: The researchers tasted the stew and found that it had a mild taste and inspired calm. Knowing that dishes were sometimes named for their intended effects, they then theorized that the name unwinding referred to the dish's relaxing function.

steps.title: Reject contrast and similarity
steps.content: Choice B would introduce an alternate possibility, but the second sentence presents a conclusion from the tasting. Choice C signals contrast, and Choice D signals a similar parallel case. The needed transition marks the theory as a result of the observed calming effect. The answer is A.
